user_query: I'm putting together an evidence-based shortlist of current synthesizers to recommend to clients. Using the files under input/, please: 1) Programmatically discover and parse every .html synthesizer spec page under input/catalog/ (do not hardcode file names). For each product page, extract at minimum: model, brand, release_year (as an integer), synthesis (e.g., Analog/Digital/Hybrid), polyphony (as an integer), keys (as an integer), and the HTML-stated price in USD (as a number). 2) Cross-validate each extracted price against input/pricing/prices.csv by joining on model; include both values and a boolean price_match flag (true if equal after parsing USD numbers). 3) Merge in inventory from input/inventory/stock.json (stock > 0 means available) and demand from input/sales/sales_2024_q1.csv (units_sold_q1_2024). 4) Build a single normalized dataset and save it to output/catalog_parsed.csv with columns: model, brand, release_year, synthesis, polyphony, keys, price_html_usd, price_csv_usd, price_match, units_sold_q1_2024, stock. 5) Filter to the latest, in-stock options: release_year >= 2023, polyphony >= 8, and stock > 0. For the filtered set, compute a ranking score and produce a top picks list. Define score as: score = 0.5*polyphony_norm + 0.3*units_sold_norm + 0.2*analog_or_hybrid_flag, where polyphony_norm and units_sold_norm are min-max normalized across the filtered set (if max equals min, treat the normalized value as 1 for that field), and analog_or_hybrid_flag = 1 if synthesis contains "Analog" or "Hybrid" (case-insensitive), else 0. Sort descending by score; break ties by lower price_csv_usd, then by model name alphabetically. Save the ranked results to output/top_picks.csv with columns: rank (1-based), model, score (rounded to 3 decimals), release_year, synthesis, polyphony, price_csv_usd, units_sold_q1_2024, stock. 6) To show directory inspection, also write output/discovery_log.txt listing each parsed HTML file path and the model extracted on one line per file. 7) Write output/price_mismatches.csv with rows for any models where price_match is false (include model, price_html_usd, price_csv_usd). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
